rules: [P1] Explicitly confirm and document search scope, parameters, and calculated duration logic when concluding data absence.
[P1] Prioritize reconciliation and state-verification over additive actions; confirm if existing orders fulfill clinical requirements before initiating new interventions.
[P1] Validate baseline history and perform comprehensive active-order searches before initiating new prescriptive actions.
[P1] When clinical protocols mandate a multi-parameter assessment, document the availability status of each required parameter individually before attempting to trigger protocol-based actions.
[P1] Cross-reference therapeutic regimens against safety co-prescriptions, prophylaxis, and mandatory adjuncts; map identified risks to existing protective measures before assuming gaps.
[P1] Explicitly communicate missing data dependencies that prevent protocol execution to ensure transparency in clinical decision-making.
[P1] When a scoped query returns empty results, perform a broader, multi-category, or keyword-agnostic search to distinguish between scope-constrained data and absolute data absence.
[P1] Formulate clinical conclusions by explicitly comparing quantitative data against both normal reference intervals and action-triggering thresholds, citing temporal relevance for all values.
[P1] Apply server-side filtering to constrain query results to relevant temporal windows and avoid exceeding context limits.
[P1] Verify patient encounter status and data availability before assuming a 'current time' anchor.
[P1] Prioritize targeted retrieval of specific domain endpoints over summary views to eliminate false negatives.
[P1] Flag and investigate anomalous or future-dated records; avoid inferring clinical status from isolated/outdated records without current evidence.
[P1] Distinguish between the mere presence of a clinical entity and the specific criteria (e.g., duration, status) required to trigger a workflow action.
[P1] Perform explicit chronological validation of timestamps against current system time before initiating state-changing actions.
[P1] Perform pharmacological class verification for all active medications rather than relying on generic name recognition; evaluate orders based on therapeutic intent and clinical class.
[P1] Report specific documentation sources when confirming presence or absence of clinical data.
[P1] Verify all temporal constraints (e.g., duration, frequency) against source timestamps before initiating conditional interventions.
[P1] Calculate exact temporal deltas and normalize reference dates/formats before performing temporal comparisons.
[P1] Distinguish between 'reporting findings' and 'performing clinical actions'; conclude tasks by initiating explicit system function calls for protocol-mandated orders.
